evals.expected_output: An empty/help response that applies the classification gate before the decomposition gate.
assertions: Agent dispatches to the empty/help handler.
Agent shows the public entry paths: empty/help, pattern, tier, and recovery.
Agent explains that the classification gate runs before the decomposition gate.
Agent names near-miss cases such as single-action and same-file sequential work.

evals.expected_output: A response explaining Pattern B using the pattern reference.
assertions: Agent dispatches to the named pattern mode.
Agent reads references/patterns.md for the detailed pattern content.
Agent explains when Pattern B is appropriate and why Pattern E remains the default.

evals.expected_output: A tier-selection response grounded in runtime boundaries.
assertions: Agent dispatches to tier mode.
Agent explains that the highest applicable tier wins.
Agent names the main reasons to stay single-session or use a flat subagent wave.

evals.expected_output: A recovery response grounded in the accounting rule and recovery ladder.
assertions: Agent dispatches to recovery mode.
Agent explains the accounting rule.
Agent explains resume, re-spawn, re-assign, and escalate behavior.
Agent makes it explicit that the next wave cannot start before unresolved lanes are reconciled.

evals.prompt: Use /orchestrator to rename one function and update its one test in the same file.
evals.expected_output: A refusal to invoke orchestrator for a single tightly-coupled action.
assertions: Agent recognizes the request as effectively one serialized action.
Agent does not force parallelism.
Agent keeps the work in a single session.

evals.prompt: Split this same-file refactor into three parallel lanes: simplify, patch, and review.
evals.expected_output: A response that rejects parallelism because the work overlaps on one file.
assertions: Agent identifies same-file overlap as a sequential constraint.
Agent explains why same-file work should remain serialized.
Agent does not recommend a parallel topology.

evals.prompt: Use a phase-gated skill with an explore phase and an implement phase, but parallelize across both phases immediately.
evals.expected_output: A response preserving the phase-gated skill's precedence.
assertions: Agent states that phase-gated skills keep their phase boundaries.
Agent allows parallelism only within a phase, not across phase boundaries.
Agent preserves the active skill's stricter control flow.
